overview: Nykthos Ramp is a Mono-Green deck that leverages Devotion to Green and massive mana acceleration to flood the board with creatures quickly. Though it lost its previous combo-focused identity after Karn, the Great Creator was banned, it now functions as a consistent ramp-combo hybrid that aims to win on explosive turns. The deck relies on a core engine of Nykthos, Shrine to Nyx, dorks, and board-fillers to overwhelm opponents before they can stabilize.
win_conditions: Generate massive mana via Nykthos, Shrine to Nyx and high Devotion to Green
Flood the board with creatures using Storm the Festival and Kiora, Behemoth Beckoner
Overwhelm opponents with a massive army to deal lethal damage in a single turn

keep_criteria: Hands containing Nykthos, mana dorks, and card draw to find key pieces
Hands that can reach Devotion 5 by turn 2
mulligan_criteria: Hands that rely heavily on future land drops
Hands with only one land and no early acceleration or Devotion

examples.reason: Has Nykthos, mana dorks, and ways to find important pieces, though it might end up too slow if you don't draw more Devotion.
Gives Devotion 5 as early as turn 2 and accelerates the game plan significantly with Kiora.
Relies too much on the next land drops and has a huge chance of getting stuck with the first land.

early_game: Play mana dorks like Llanowar Elves on turn 1 to accelerate
Prioritize lands and Devotion creatures like Cavalier of Thorns and Old-Growth Troll
Utilize Leyline of the Guildpact for explosive opening turns
mid_game: Cast Oath of Nissa and Invasion of Ixalan to dig for Nykthos or Kiora
Use Storm the Festival to generate tokens and additional mana
Stabilize against aggro by blocking with Old-Growth Troll to create mana
late_game: Activate Kiora, Behemoth Beckoner to attack with the entire board
Overwhelm opponents with massive creature counts and mana advantage
Deploy sideboard hate pieces like Tranquil Frillback or Pithing Needle to shut down specific strategies

key_cards.name: Nykthos, Shrine to Nyx
key_cards.notes: Names the list and generates massive mana when Devotion to Green is high; without it, the deck is just expensive green cards.

key_cards.name: Kiora, Behemoth Beckoner
key_cards.notes: Lets you attack with everything on the board, representing lethal damage in nearly all situations.

key_cards.name: Storm the Festival
key_cards.notes: Creates tokens and generates mana to accelerate the game plan and fill the board even more.

key_cards.name: Llanowar Elves
key_cards.notes: Accelerates the mana curve and improves Kiora's effectiveness by enabling earlier plays.

key_cards.name: Cavalier of Thorns
key_cards.notes: Provides Devotion to Green and can search for Nykthos or create more mana by keeping a new copy in play.

key_cards.name: Leyline of the Guildpact
key_cards.notes: Replaced Wolfwillow Haven to provide explosiveness in the first few turns, even if it can be dead later.

sequencing_tips: Play mana dorks as early as possible to accelerate into Devotion creatures.
Prioritize playing Cavalier of Thorns and Old-Growth Troll quickly to maximize Nykthos's mana generation.
Use Oath of Nissa and Invasion of Ixalan to dig for missing key pieces like Nykthos or Kiora.
Plan turns carefully, especially the first few, to jump ahead in mana and flood the board.

matchups.advice: Get the best opening hand and great card draw. Stick big creatures fast and maximize Old-Growth Troll's blocking ability to create mana and stabilize.
Build a solid board as fast as possible to finish the game. Use Cavalier of Thorns to deal with flying attacks, but focus on overwhelming them quickly.
Use their slow start to develop your game plan. Don't waste opportunities to deal damage and always play around removals and counters.
Make good choices and play the right cards at the right times. The player with the best starting hand and draws will win quickly in this combo mirror.
Look for hands with lots of creatures to progress your plan while they discard. Overcome their value in the early turns to mow them down.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: The current list is really similar to the classic 2019 version, which played a lot of ramp so you could put your threats in play faster... Though this deck lost its combo-focused side, it still plays like a combo.

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: Nykthos is the card that names this list - without it, Mono-Green is only a green deck full of expensive cards. However, to get the most out of this land, you need Devotion
Storm the Festival will fill the board for you, and many times let you create even more mana - which, in turn, means you'll be able to fill the board even more.

This creature is basically a finisher in this list, as it lets you attack with everything you have after you throw your entire deck on the board. It represents a lethal amount of damage in nearly all situations.

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: Cavalier of Thorns and Troll will give you the Devotion you need, so you should play them as fast as possible.
You'll usually play them to find Nykthos, Kiora, or another piece.
Your goal is to jump ahead in mana so you can put as many creatures as possible in play. For this reason, you must plan your turns really well, particularly your first few turns.
